mnemonics: **S.P.A.M. Filter** - Use a parsimonious model to filter out Seasonal data without getting bogged down in too many Parameters. **S**easonal, **P**arsimonious, **A**utoregressive, **M**oving Average
keyPoints: Parsimonious models use fewer parameters than full seasonal ARIMA models by incorporating seasonal differencing and a single seasonal AR or MA term.
A common parsimonious model is the Airline Model: ARIMA(0,1,1)(0,1,1)_s, which uses only two parameters (non-seasonal and seasonal MA terms) to model a wide range of seasonal behavior.
The primary goal is to achieve a good fit and accurate forecasts while minimizing model complexity to avoid overfitting.
commonMistakes: Over-differencing the series, both seasonally and non-seasonally, which can lead to unnecessary complexity and invertibility issues.
Adding extra AR or MA terms (seasonal or non-seasonal) when a simpler model (like the Airline Model) would suffice, violating the principle of parsimony.
